items.title: Building one ROC point at threshold t
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  scoresNode["scores s and labels y"]
  threshNode["threshold t: predict 1 if s >= t"]
  countsNode["count TP, FP, TN, FN"]
  ratesNode["TPR = TP/(TP+FN), FPR = FP/(FP+TN)"]
  pointNode["one point (FPR, TPR) on ROC"]
  scoresNode --> threshNode
  threshNode --> countsNode
  countsNode --> ratesNode
  ratesNode --> pointNode
items.caption: Each threshold induces a confusion-matrix-style split; TPR and FPR become one ROC coordinate.

items.title: Sweeping thresholds along the ROC curve
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  highNode["high t: few positives, low TPR and FPR"]
  midNode["lower t: more TP and FP"]
  lowNode["very low t: TPR=1, FPR=1"]
  curveNode["connect points -> ROC curve"]
  highNode --> midNode
  midNode --> lowNode
  lowNode --> curveNode
items.caption: Moving the cutoff from strict to permissive traces the ROC from (0,0) toward (1,1).

items.title: AUC as ranking quality
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  posNode["positive examples"]
  negNode["negative examples"]
  rankNode["sort all by score s descending"]
  compareNode["compare random pos vs neg score"]
  aucNode["AUC = fraction pos ranks above neg"]
  posNode --> rankNode
  negNode --> rankNode
  rankNode --> compareNode
  compareNode --> aucNode
items.caption: High AUC means positives systematically receive higher scores than negatives — threshold-free separability.
